text:
There once was a young fox who loved nothing more than to leap through the night among the bramble bushes. Every evening after a long day foraging, he admired the shimmering moonlit world.

One night, as he lounged among the shadows, he heard rustling. Curious, he crept closer. It was a family of moths weaving a silken web. The young fox's eyes twinkled. Perhaps there'd be some silk to play with?

Quietly, he snatched a small thread and danced along the web. Suddenly, a bat swooped down, startled. The fox laughed joyously and darted away on a twirl.

The next day, the moths were angry. They fluttered around him angrily.

"The audacity!" one hissed.

"A fox?" the others chuffed. "We'll teach him to respect our work."

Together, they crafted a trap from their own silken threads. When night came, the fox hopped in.
